title: Where to Eat Between San Francisco and Los Angeles
author: Rebecca Roland
summary: There are a handful of routes connecting the Bay Area and Los Angeles, each offering wildly different options in terms of natural beauty and amenities for travelers. You can spend a long day (or a leisurely weekend) winding down Highway 1, stopping along the way at a handful of charming seaside enclaves, each with its own culinary destinations. Or prio
full_text:
Harris Ranch Inn & Restaurant

Once you roll up to this sprawling hotel, restaurant, and market complex, you’re almost exactly halfway between Los Angeles and the Bay Area. Celebrate this achievement with a country breakfast in the Ranch Kitchen, a pub burger and a pint at the adults-only Horseshoe Bar, or a bone-in prime rib at the more buttoned-up Prime Steakhouse. Harris Ranch has a restaurant to suit every taste, plus a general store with snacks, baked goods, sandwiches, and a seemingly endless array of tchotchkes. During peak breakfast and lunch hours, be prepared to wait a bit at the Ranch Kitchen, the most popular of the three establishments.
